statement.p: Your savings account starts with \$500\text{.} Then each month, there is an automatic deposit of \$150\text{.} You need \$1700 to afford a deposit on a new apartment. How long will this take?

solution.p: With any word problem, it is best to clearly define the variable you will use once you really understand what the question is asking for. In this case, we let t (for time) be the number of months until you have saved up \$1700\text{.}
To set up an equation, we might start by making a table in order to identify a general pattern for the total amount in the savings account after t months.
We find the pattern is that after t months, the total amount saved is 150t + 500\text{.} Using this pattern, we set up the equation
to represent when we will have saved up \$1700\text{.} To solve this equation, we start by subtracting 500 from each side. Then we can divide each side by 150\text{.}
So 8 is the solution, and it checks out. This means it will take 8 months for the account balance to reach \$1700\text{.}

webwork-reps.pg:
############################################# ### Generated from PreTeXt source ### ### https://pretextbook.org ### ############################################# ## DBsubject() ## DBchapter() ## DBsection() ## Level() ## KEYWORDS() ## TitleText1() ## EditionText1() ## AuthorText1() ## Section1(not reported) ## Problem1(1.2.3) ## Author() ## Institution() ## Language(en-US)
DOCUMENT();
############################################################ # Load Macros ############################################################ loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "PGML.pl", "AnswerFormatHelp.pl", "niceTables.pl", "parserNumberWithUnits.pl", "PGcourse.pl", ); COMMENT('Authored in PreTeXt');
############################################################ # Header ############################################################ TEXT(beginproblem());
############################################################ # PG Setup Code ############################################################ Context('Numeric'); $t = NumberWithUnits("8 months");
############################################################ # Body ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML Your savings account starts with [`\$500\text{.}`] Then each month, there is an automatic deposit of [`\$150\text{.}`] You need [`\$1700`] to afford a deposit on a new apartment. How long will this take?
[_]{$t}{16}
END_PGML
############################################################ # Solution ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION With any word problem, it is best to clearly define the variable you will use once you really understand what the question is asking for. In this case, we let [`t`] (for time) be the number of months until you have saved up [`\$1700\text{.}`]
To set up an equation, we might start by making a table in order to identify a general pattern for the total amount in the savings account after [`t`] months.
>> [@DataTable( [ [[PGML('Time Passed (in Months) '), rowbottom => '2', midrule => '1',], PGML('Account Balance (in Dollars) '), ], [PGML('[`0`]'), PGML('[`500`]'), ], [PGML('[`1`]'), PGML('[`650`] ([`150`] more than [`500`])'), ], [PGML('[`2`]'), PGML('[`800`] ([`300`] more than [`500`])'), ], [PGML('[`3`]'), PGML('[`950`] ([`450`] more than [`500`])'), ], [PGML('[`4`]'), PGML('[`1100`] ([`600`] more than [`500`])'), ], [PGML('[`\vdots`]'), [PGML('[`\vdots`]'), halign => 'c',], ], ], align => 'cl', valign => 'middle',);@]* <<
We find the pattern is that after [`t`] months, the total amount saved is [`150t + 500\text{.}`] Using this pattern, we set up the equation
[``` 150t + 500 = 1700 ```]
to represent when we will have saved up [`\$1700\text{.}`] To solve this equation, we start by subtracting [`500`] from each side. Then we can divide each side by [`150\text{.}`]
[```\newcommand{\subtractright}[1]{{\highlight{{{}-#1}}}}\newcommand{\divideunder}[2]{\frac{#1}{{\highlight{{#2}}}}}\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} 500+150m\amp=1700\\ 500+150m\subtractright{500}\amp=1700\subtractright{500}\\ 150m\amp=1200\\ \divideunder{150m}{150}\amp=\divideunder{1200}{150}\\ m\amp=8 \end{aligned} ```]
Checking the solution [`8\text{:}`]
[```\newcommand{\substitute}[1]{{\color{blue}{{#1}}}}\newcommand{\confirm}[1]{\stackrel{\checkmark}{#1}}\newcommand{\wonder}[1]{\stackrel{\text{?}}{#1}}\newcommand{\amp}{&} \begin{aligned} 500+150m \amp = 1700\\ 500+150(\substitute{8})\amp \wonder{=}1700\\ 500+1200 \amp\confirm{=}1700 \end{aligned} ```]
So [`8`] is the solution, and it checks out. This means it will take [`8`] months for the account balance to reach [`\$1700\text{.}`]
END_PGML_SOLUTION
############################################################ # End Problem ############################################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
